title: When Using AI Leads to “Brain Fry”
subtitle: A new study finds that certain patterns of AI use are driving cognitive fatigue, while others can help reduce burnout.

content_blocks.title: The Impact of AI Overload on Workers

items.label: Of participants reported experiencing 'AI brain fry'
Increase in decision fatigue for those with brain fry
Increase in major error frequency for those experiencing brain fry
Lower burnout scores when AI replaces routine tasks

content_blocks.title: AI Brain Fry vs. Workplace Burnout

items.a: Acute mental fatigue caused by excessive cognitive load and oversight.
items.b: Chronic workplace stress consisting of exhaustion and negative feelings.

items.a: Symptoms include mental 'fog,' 'buzzing,' and slower decision-making.
items.b: Symptoms include emotional exhaustion and decreased job effectiveness.

items.a: Worsened by intensive AI monitoring and multitasking (3+ tools).
items.b: Alleviated when AI replaces routine or repetitive 'toil'.

content_blocks.title: Strategic Lessons for Leaders
steps: Redesign jobs and tools holistically for shared human and AI responsibility.
Set explicit expectations regarding AI's role in total workload and productivity.
Shift performance metrics from activity and intensity to actual business impact.
Develop worker skills specifically for managing and prioritizing AI workloads.
Monitor and safeguard human attention as a finite, strategic resource.
